definition: Freedom from aversion, expressed as non-hostility, patience, and kindness.
notes: The project keeps non-aversion as the default because it preserves the lexical relation to dosa and works well in analytical contexts. Kindness can be useful in explanatory prose, but it is more a common expression of adosa than a complete doctrinal equivalent.

context_rules.context: In prose emphasizing warm practical expression
context_rules.notes: Acceptable alternate when the ethical quality is being expressed rather than classified.

context_rules.context: In prose stressing the absence of hostility
context_rules.notes: Useful when the contrast with aversion needs emphasis.

example_phrases.translation: non-aversion is a root of the wholesome

example_phrases.notes: Headword form often appears cleanly in analytical lists.
